text: ATP is the rechargeable energy currency your cells spend constantly. Every cell uses ATP to fuel functions, from muscle contractions to nerve impulses. But how is this tiny molecule so powerful? Let's dive deeper. ATP is created in mitochondria, often called the powerhouse of the cell. It’s not just energy; ATP regulates metabolism, signaling, and cell growth. Want to unlock more cellular secrets? Follow for more science insights!
